calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Kingston Hom (Executor/Son). William Wong for Winston Hom (Son) and Lai Chun Ng Hom (Spouse).

Ms. Ng presented argument stating that the papers speak for themselves, that Winston knew of the title change, that he never made any issue while his Father was alive, and that they believe he is barred. In addition, she addressed the spousal petition/will contest, stated that the homestead petition was denied a few weeks ago, addressed the substance of Ms. Hom's claim stating that the property has always been in the decedent's name, addressed his Estate planning and intention to disinherit Ms. Hom, stated that Ms. Hom has never been on title, that there is no community property, and that the Estate should be distributed as filed. Mr. Wong stated that he and Ms. Ng had a stipulation to continue this matter two weeks, that Ms. Ng then filed a different stipulation, and that Ms. Ng wasn't sending him the paperwork. In addition, Mr. Wong argued stating that his client has a peasant background, that her husband secretly disinherited her, that they believe the money is Winstons, and that the fraud statute would not run. The Court took a brief recess on this matter. At the second call of the matter, Counsel presented additional arguments to support their respective positions. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR#11218. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Continued To Mar-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Payment Of Family Allowance
Off Calendar Mar-12-2010 Denied

Petition For Order Setting Aside A Probate Homestead
Off Calendar Mar-12-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Kingston Hom. William Wong for Winston Hom and Lai Chung Ng Hom.

The Court requested Counsel address the decendent's wife's petition. Ms. Ng stated that they had filed objections, stated that Mrs. Hom lives with her adult Son, that Mrs. Hom had filed a spousal petition and will contest, that nothing happened with those petitions, that there was no point in granting her special privileges, that this has gone on for three years, and that they are ready to close. Mr. Wong argued that Mrs. Hom was born in China, that she doesn't speak English, that she took care of her husband, that there are issues with the step-children, that Mrs. Hom talks loud, but she isn't abusive. In addition, Mr. Wong presented the Court with a copies of the declarations that he filed today. The Court informed Mr. Wong that these declarations were late. Mr. Wong stated that they just made additional discoveries in the last couple of days, that they believe the children have lived in one of the flats rent free, presented issues with the rents that were paid over the last couple of weeks, stated that no rents were paid during the administration, and that the rents were irrational, etc. Mr. Wong also addressed the $90,000.00 that disappeared stating that Winston Hom was distracted at the time he signed the back of the check. Ms. Ng stated that she just received Mr. Wong's declarations before court, that Mrs. Hom hasn't verified her need for funds, and that this goes against what the decendent wanted. The Court took the homestead and family allowance petitions under submission and continued the petition for final distribution to March 14, 2010.

Off the record: the petition for final distribution was continued to March 15, 2010.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-25-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 289911. Lillian Ng appearing for and with petitioner Kingston Hom. William Wong appearing for and with surviving spouse Lai chung Ng Hom. Court will issue an order consolidating these two cases to the lower number. Mr. Wong wanted to do further discovery before filing Will Contest. He will amend his spousal property petition to include the wife's 38 years contribution to the real property. Ms. Ng objected to the continuance of her petition for probate. Court is appointing Kingston Hom as the executor with limited authority. Bond is fixed in the amount of $250,000.00. Mr. Wong objected to the appointment and he wanted to have the Public Administrator appointed. It appeared to the Court that he is objecting to the conservatorship. Ms. Ng will file the final account in the conservatorship. Mr. Wong can object to the conservator's final account. Order appointing executor with limited authority with Bond fixed in the amount of $250,000.00 signed.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Tony Santiago.
